name: Indian IT Sector (TCS/Infosys/Wipro)
summary: Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session [May jobs + AI rout] -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange). **THE AI-CAPEX DOUBT WENT MAINSTREAM — A DOUBLE-EDGED EVENT FOR INDIAN IT SERVICES; THE SECTION 301 12.5% TARIFF REMAINS A CONTESTABLE PROPOSAL INDIA IS NEGOTIATING.** The 5-June US session cracked the crowded AI/semi trade hard (Broadcom's AI-guidance miss + a hot May jobs print 172K vs ~85K -> SMH -9.2%, Nasdaq -4%, VIX +39.7% to ~21.5) — a rates/AI de-risking. For Indian IT services this is DOUBLE-EDGED: a repricing of AI-capex justification can (a) relieve the 'AI-flows-bypass-Indian-services' overhang at the margin (the builders trade is the one cracking, not the adopters), yet (b) a US-demand/recession scare from the same hot-jobs/higher-for-longer rates read is a headwind for discretionary IT spend. The continued FII/IT/oil drag persists. The US Section 301 12.5% tariff on India remains a PROPOSAL (within a ~54-economy action; comments ~22 Jun, hearings 7 Jul, decision late-Jun/Jul; India negotiating it into the trade pact) — a non-oil headwind that does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal but is contingent/contestable, not a levied duty. The structural cycle-bear is unchanged but the AI-builders crack is the first event that even partially questions the bypass thesis. The profit-warning trigger stays watching (no company guidance cut). PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **THE 'IT STOCK SHOCK' RESOLVED — THE 3-JUNE NIFTY IT -5.57% PLUNGE STABILISED; THE 12.5% TARIFF REFINED TO A 54-COUNTRY PROPOSAL INDIA IS NEGOTIATING.** The 3 June IT-led selloff (Nifty IT -5.57% on profit-booking layered on the US-tariff overhang) resolved into a 4-June India stabilisation (Nifty +0.1%). The Section 301 tariff refined materially (intel A12): it is a 54-country action (China ALSO 12.5%, India not singled out), a PROPOSAL with comments 22 Jun / hearings 7 Jul / decision late-Jun-Jul, and India is disputing it (GTRI) and seeking to fold it into the interim trade pact — contingent and contestable, not a levied duty. The structural bear is unchanged: AI flows keep bypassing Indian services for the Korea/Taiwan/US tech stack — though TODAY that AI trade took its first valuation crack (KOSPI -5.3%), a potential medium-term tailwind for EM/India flows. The tariff does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal; the profit-warning trigger stays watching (price/tariff-driven, no company guidance cut). PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers to 3 June morning). **THE RUPEE-REVENUE TAILWIND FLIPPED TO A HEADWIND — A NEW US TARIFF VECTOR DROVE AN 'IT STOCK SHOCK' COMPONENT OF THE 2-3 JUNE INDIA SELLOFF.** The defining new development for the sector is the USTR-proposed 12.5% Section 301 tariff on India (vs 10% for EU/UK/Canada/Mexico) — surfacing as a named driver of the 2 June rupee/Nifty weakness (The Week + Bloomberg). For Indian IT specifically, the tariff threat + a renewed US-demand-risk frame produced an 'IT stock shock' (markets C4/A7) — inverting the prior 'rupee weakness = exporter tailwind' read that had supported IT through May. The structural cycle-bear case is unchanged and now compounded: (1) AI flows continue to bypass Indian services for the Korea/Taiwan/US tech stack (SMH +26.1% MoM, KOSPI +31.5% MoM); (2) a US tariff/earnings-risk overhang on top. India is seeking a bilateral resolution on the tariff, but until resolved it is a non-oil, non-crisis headwind that does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal. The profit-warning trigger stays watching (no formal warning yet — the move is price/tariff-driven, not a company guidance cut). PRIOR — Day 78. **B7 INDIAN IT-PACK STRENGTH IS THE CYCLE-INTERNAL ROTATION SIGNAL — Infosys +1.92%, Tech Mahindra +1.79%** among the top gainers when Sensex closed May 15 -0.21% / Nifty -0.19%. **Interpretation**: US recession risk priced through Indian IT services (rupee-revenue tailwind from USD/INR ₹95.96 + flight-to-defensive within Indian equity bucket). This is the FIRST cycle session where Indian IT outperformed in a global stagflation-shock session — possibly the first sign of the AI-cycle-decoupling-from-India headwind beginning to invert, but more likely a one-session rupee-revenue defensive bid. **Nifty Pharma defensive bid intact; Nifty IT remains -3% YTD vs Nifty -14% YTD = the cycle-bear case on Indian IT is structural (US AI-pricing-pressure + AI-cycle bypassing Indian services), but the May 15 session is an internal-rotation signal worth tracking.** SMH $556.34 -3.8% 1D — Indian IT diverged from US tech selloff on the day, which is unusual and supports the rupee-revenue-defensive read. NVDA earnings May 20 next major catalyst. PRIOR (May 7): **AI-CYCLE DECOUPLING FROM INDIA STRUCTURALLY HARDENS — KOSPI +37.3% / TAIEX +26.2% / SMH +37.5% (1M) WHILE NIFTY UNDERPERFORMS RISK-ON.** Apple-Samsung-Intel chip-production talks (Bloomberg May 5, CNBC corroborated) extend halo to Korea/Taiwan/US tech stack, NOT to Indian IT services. Samsung Q1 net profits 57.2T KRW (~$39B) — single quarter higher than ALL of 2025 combined; Samsung market cap crossed $1T May 6 on KOSPI 7,400 (+6.7%). Nikkei +5.7% Golden Week catch-up (SoftBank +13-16%, Advantest +8%, Tokyo Electron +9%) is international validation of AI-cycle decoupling. **For India deployment thesis:** AI flows continue to bypass India IT services and rotate to Korea/Taiwan/US tech — Indian-equity allocation continues to underperform structurally in this regime. Patel/SemiAnalysis: May 4-6 sequence is structural confirmation; Apple-Samsung-Intel talks specifically signal a US-AI-manufacturing-onshore tailwind that will rotate flows further from Indian IT services. Q4 FY26 earnings weakness (Infosys, HDFC, Tech Mahindra, Wipro, HCL Tech) remains dominant DOMESTIC driver. The peace-dividend session today did NOT lift Indian IT — direct-vs-indirect bifurcation persists.

active_triggers.condition: Major Indian IT company issues profit warning citing US demand slowdown

top_edges.context: 2026-06-06: the AI-builders trade (the engine of the flows-bypass-Indian-services dynamic) took its biggest crack yet (SMH -9.2%, Broadcom AI-guidance miss). Double-edged: marginal relief of the bypass overhang vs a US-demand/recession scare for discretionary IT spend. Weight held at 7.5 (directness-1; strict formula ~5×1.0×3.0 caps at 10 but the read is double-edged/unconfirmed — kept stable).

recent_signals.headline: Indian IT the main Nifty drag on 11 Jun (Infosys -2.25%) on US-recession/CPI worry -- the sector most exposed to the US macro deterioration (CPI 4.2%, December-hike repricing, recession framing) rather than to the Gulf kinetics. The transmission to India is via the US demand/rates channel, not the war.

**THE AI-CAPEX DOUBT WENT MAINSTREAM — DOUBLE-EDGED FOR INDIAN IT SERVICES; THE 12.5% SECTION 301 TARIFF REMAINS A CONTESTABLE PROPOSAL INDIA IS NEGOTIATING.** The 5-June US session cracked the crowded AI/semi trade hard (Broadcom AI-guidance miss + hot May jobs 172K vs ~85K -> SMH -9.2%, Nasdaq -4%, VIX +39.7% to ~21.5) — a rates/AI de-risking. For Indian IT services this is DOUBLE-EDGED: the AI-builders trade (the engine of the 'AI-flows-bypass-Indian-services' overhang) is the one cracking, which marginally relieves that overhang; but the same hot-jobs/higher-for-longer rates read carries a US-demand/recession scare that is a headwind for discretionary IT spend. The continued FII/IT/oil drag persists. The US Section 301 12.5% tariff on India remains a PROPOSAL (within a ~54-economy action; comments ~22 Jun, hearings 7 Jul, decision late-Jun/Jul; India negotiating it into the trade pact) — a non-oil headwind that does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal but is contingent/contestable, not levied. The structural cycle-bear is unchanged; the profit-warning trigger stays watching (no company guidance cut).

recent_signals.headline: **INDIAN IT BENEFITS FROM RUPEE WEAKNESS — TCS, INFOSYS, HCL, TECH MAHINDRA, ORACLE, PERSISTENT UP 3.4-4.5% INTRADAY ON ₹96.44 + POSTPONEMENT RELIEF.** Bharti Airtel +1.66%. **Mechanism**: USD-denominated revenue translates better at weaker rupee — IT exporter idiosyncratic positive within structurally bearish India tape. **Classic 'good news for indices, bad news for the underlying economy' Indian-market schizophrenia** — IT exporters catch the postponement-relief while fuel pass-through fires and rupee weakens to fresh record low.
recent_signals.verification: IT stocks +3-4.5% intraday = REPORTED (TradingEconomics single-source); rupee-IT-exporter mechanism = CONFIRMED structural.
